title: PDMS Fabrication
author: Kenneth Schackart
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yadfsa6

steps.instruction: Wipe the inside of the petri dish lid with 70% ethanol solution using a Kimwipe® to rid the surface of debris.
Place petri dish lid(s) and a 10 cm × 10 cm piece of foil in the dessicator, in the fume hood.
Add 2 µL Tridecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrooctyl trichlorosilane to the piece of aluminum foil. Chemical is highly corrosive, do not inhale or get on skin.
Dessicate under vacuum for 00:15:00 to vaporize reagent. Press down on the lid as you apply vacuum. Once vacuum is achieved the lid should be able to be lifted.
Add 100 µL PDMS curing agent and 900 µL PDMS polymer to 2 mL tube.
Mix the solution with an inoculation loop until there are small bubbles throughout the solution. This will take approximately 00:05:00 .
Pour the mixed PDMS into petri dish, and try to get all of the polymer out of the tube using a pipette tip or inoculation loop. Evenly spread the polymer in the petri dish by tilting.
Place petri dish on hot plate at 120 °C for 00:10:00 covered in aluminum foil.
Using a blade, cut out a piece about 1 cm × 1 cm and set on glass microscope slide. This will be your PDMS surface for water contact angle analysis.
